title: Maternal mortality ratio (modeled estimate, per 100,000 live births)
definition: Maternal mortality ratio is the number of women who die from pregnancy-related causes while pregnant or within 42 days of pregnancy termination per 100,000 live births. The data are estimated with a regression model using information on the proportion of maternal deaths among non-AIDS deaths in women ages 15-49, fertility, birth attendants, and GDP measured using purchasing power parities (PPPs).

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: A stark map of childbirth risk.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Nigeria, Chad, South Sudan, while the lightest include Belarus, Norway, Australia; that contrast is the fastest way to read the maternal mortality pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Maternal mortality condenses health-system access, emergency care, and broader development into a readable but serious signal.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Infant mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Under-5 mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Neonatal mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Strong, understandable health-system map with clear high and low anchors.
Works for Daily when not paired with multiple child-mortality indicators.
